article_title: Pfizer's Sigvotatug Vedotin Misses Overall Survival Goal in Phase 3 Lung Cancer Trial, Salvaging a Signal in Second-Line Patients
summary: Negative/mixed Phase 3 readout accurately framed as an OS miss; every quote, name, and figure verified verbatim against the cited Pfizer release; no fabricated efficacy numbers. Only finding is a non-blocking allowlist note.

content_preview.title: Pfizer's Sigvotatug Vedotin Misses Overall Survival Goal in Phase 3 Lung Cancer Trial, Salvaging a Signal in Second-Line Patients
content_preview.summary: Pfizer's integrin beta-6 ADC failed to beat docetaxel on overall survival across the full SigVie-002 population, but showed a stronger trend in the two-thirds of patients with one prior line of therapy.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Pfizer's experimental antibody-drug conjugate sigvotatug vedotin failed to extend overall survival across the full population of a Phase 3 lung cancer trial, the company announced on June 22, 2026. According to [Pfizer](https://www.pfizer.com/news/press-release/press-release-detail/pfizer-announces-topline-phase-3-results-sigvotatug-vedotin), the drug "did not show a statistically significant improvement in the primary endpoint of overall survival (OS) compared to docetaxel" in the ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, disciplined News piece on a negative/mixed Phase 3 readout. Standard Overview / What We Know / What Comes Next / What We Don't Know structure, 642 words, appropriate for the News category. The headline and lead correctly center the OS miss in the full SigVie-002 (Be6A Lung-01) NSCLC population and characterize the one-prior-line subgroup only as a 'stronger trend' and a salvageable signal, not as a win.
editor_notes.source_verification: Personally read all three gzipped snapshots from disk (sources/2026-06/pfizers-sigvotatug-vedotin-misses-overall-survival-goal-in-phase-3-lung-cancer-trial-salvaging-a-signal-in-second-line-patients/). All three returned HTTP 200 and the uncompressed sha256 of each file matches manifest.json. source-0 (Pfizer press release): confirmed verbatim the SigVie-002/Be6A Lung-01 naming, 'investigational, potential first-in-class integrin beta-6 (IB6) directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC)', docetaxel comparator, 'did not show a statistically significant improvement in the primary endpoint of overall survival (OS) compared to docetaxel', the 'stronger trend was observed for OS and progression-free survival (PFS)' in the one-prior-line subgroup that 'represents two-thirds of the study population', the full Jeff Legos and Solange Peters quotes verbatim, 'the safety profile of sigvotatug vedotin was manageable and consistent with prior studies', 'Detailed results from SigVie-002 will be submitted for presentation at a future medical congress', the 1L pembrolizumab Phase 3 (PD-L1 TPS >=50%), the PD-1/VEGF bispecific combination, and the Monday June 22, 2026 dateline. source-1 (BioSpace, in allowlist): confirmed the OS-miss quote verbatim. source-2 (Investing News): confirmed 'is expressed on approximately 90% of tumors', 'associated with poor prognosis', the Seagen-acquisition ADC-portfolio sentence verbatim, and 'fetrastobart vedotin, a PD-L1-directed ADC currently in Phase 3 in NSCLC'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabricated efficacy numbers. The topline release deliberately did not disclose hazard ratios, confidence intervals, or magnitude of survival difference, and the article does not invent any — it correctly flags them as undisclosed in 'What We Don't Know'. The press release discloses enrollment (703) and the NCT number, which the article opts not to cite; stating enrollment size as undisclosed is a minor imprecision but not material to any claim (no number is overstated). Rule 9 satisfied: the headline topline result, the IB6 ADC / Seagen-origin framing, and the docetaxel comparator all rest on the non-blocked Pfizer press release.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured. No sensationalism; the company's optimistic subgroup framing is attributed to Pfizer/Legos/Peters rather than adopted as the newsroom's own. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of sigvotatug vedotin, SigVie-002, or integrin beta-6 ADCs. New topic, not a duplicate.
recommendations: pfizer.com and investingnews.com are not in config/source_allowlist.txt (biospace.com is). This is a NON-BLOCKING warning and the allowlist was intentionally left unedited. The headline-bearing claims all trace to the Pfizer first-party press release, which is the authoritative primary source for a company's own topline trial result. A maintainer may consider adding pfizer.com to the allowlist for future first-party Pfizer releases.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Accurate, well-sourced reporting of a negative/mixed Phase 3 result with no overstatement and no fabricated figures. The sole finding is a non-blocking allowlist note; no content correction is warranted, so this is a clean APPROVE rather than APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
